title: Overriding untyped parameter with -G produces incorrect result

features.name: untyped parameter declaration with implicit type from value
features.description: Code should include a parameter declared without explicit type keyword, where the type is implicitly determined from the value expression (e.g., parameter declaration without 'parameter logic' or 'parameter int' prefix)

features.name: parameter override via command-line -G flag
features.description: Code should include a parameterizable module where a parameter is intended to be overridden via verilator -G command-line flag, and the parameter affects data path widths or type-dependent operations

features.name: parameter-driven width calculation in data path
features.description: Code should include signal declarations where width is derived from parameter values, creating type-sensitive contexts where parameter type matters for correct simulation
